slides.title: An instrument is a *full brain* workout.
slides.sub: Playing music lights up motor, hearing and memory networks at the same time. Here is why that protects an aging mind.

slides.title: Many systems, one act
slides.body: Reading notes, moving your hands and listening at once asks the brain to *coordinate several networks* in real time.

slides.title: A dopamine lift
slides.body: Learning and landing a passage releases dopamine, the reward chemical that *can support* focus, motivation and mood.

slides.title: Cortisol comes down
slides.body: Time spent playing is linked to *lower cortisol*, the stress hormone that wears on the brain and body over years.

slides.title: A more resilient brain
slides.body: Regular musical practice is linked to *stronger memory* and sharper attention as we age, even when you start later in life.

slides.title: Minutes beat marathons
slides.body: A short, steady practice most days *does more than* rare long ones. Consistency is what builds the habit and the gains.

slides.title: Make the *good habits* stick.
slides.body: Vibe Health scores the sleep, food, movement and steps that keep your brain sharp, turning daily habits into one clear Health Score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Picking up an instrument is one of the most complete things you can do for your brain. 🎵🌿

It fires up motor, hearing and memory networks at once, lifts dopamine and lowers cortisol, all of which can help protect an aging mind. Save this for your next quiet afternoon. 🔖

Vibe Health turns your daily habits into one simple score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Playing an Instrument Protects Your Aging Brain
